title: pilosella glaucescens subsp. filifera
intro: Pilosella glaucescens subsp. filifera, also known as a subspecies of the plant Pilosella glaucescens, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds. The health benefits of Pilosella glaucescens subsp. filifera include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and ease respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Pilosella glaucescens subsp. filifera can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
